Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 16:33 ET
Index puts clash with single-name calls as dealers turn short gamma
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Extreme P/C divergence signals institutional dispersion trades — index 1.35 vs single-name 0.68 with dealer short gamma amplifying moves.
  • 2. UNH dominates two-way flow (+1269% calls, +1070% puts) ahead of earnings season despite cheap vol signals.
  • 3. GDP data in 3 days creates urgency for current positioning with negative SPY gamma turning every move into momentum.
NARRATIVE — bullish flow, short gamma

The most striking development is the institutional footprint in options flow — index puts trading 1.35x calls while single-name calls dominate at 0.68x puts, the widest divergence since March. This classic dispersion setup coincides with dealer short gamma of -$2.26B in SPY, meaning any directional move gets amplified rather than dampened. The combination suggests sophisticated money is hedging broad exposure while expressing single-name conviction, particularly dangerous when dealers can't provide liquidity cushion. UNH epitomizes the two-way vol demand driving today's action, with calls +1269% and puts +1070% despite trading at -20% IV discount to sector peers. The healthcare giant's unusual activity lacks obvious news catalyst, suggesting either earnings positioning or sector rotation trades ahead of GDP data Wednesday. Similarly, NVDA's massive 140K put block at 200 strike reflects institutional hedging rather than bearish conviction, given the stock's technical momentum and Millennium's recent 93% position increase. Cross-asset tension emerges in the vol surface where 11 names show backwardation (AMD, ARM, COIN leading) yet implied correlation sits at just 0.04 — the lowest stock-specific regime reading in weeks. This creates dispersion opportunity but requires precision timing with GDP data creating binary outcomes in 72 hours.

Layer 1: Sentiment +25Layer 2: GEX -$2.26BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.35/0.68Layer 4: 11 names backwardationLayer 9: GDP in 3d

NVDABEARISH
Massive put block hedge
NVDA attracted 140,829 put contracts at 200 strike despite strong technical momentum and Millennium's recent 93% Q4 position increase. The block represents institutional hedging rather than bearish conviction, given RSI overbought at 85 and golden cross intact. Timing suggests protection ahead of GDP volatility.

Apr 22 200P block, institutional hedge
BULLBULLISH
Extreme call demand spike
BULL ETF shows +1395% call volume surge with P/C at 0.07, indicating heavy directional positioning. No specific news catalyst explains the unusual activity, but the extreme call skew suggests either momentum chasing or positioning ahead of GDP data. The ETF focuses on 3x leveraged bull exposure.
